title: Guide: Getting Started with shadcn UI Builder
description: A practical guide to using shadcn UI Builder for fast design-system setup without losing code ownership, token discipline, or governance.
excerpt: Introduction  shadcn UI Builder is useful when a team wants to get from visual preferencesetting to projectowned code quickly. The important distinction is that it does not produce a hosted UI runtime. It helps configure and generate the first layer of a system that still lives inside your repository.  That makes it more valuable than many visual builders for teams who care about maintainability, but only if they keep the right boundary in mind: the builder accelerates setup, not architecture.   1. Know What Problem the Builder Is Solving

title: AI & Storytelling - Creative Coding
description: A practical guide to using AI in creative coding projects without losing authorship, narrative intent, or the structure needed for repeatable experiments.
excerpt: Introduction  Creative coding gets more interesting when AI is used as a collaborator in variation, pacing, and structure rather than as a button that produces finished work. The challenge is preserving authorship while still benefiting from generative range.  This guide outlines a practical way to build narrative or visual experiments that stay intentional.   1. Start With the Experience, Not the Model

title: AI & Developer's Journey
description: A practical guide for developers adapting to AI-native workflows without abandoning engineering judgment, testing discipline, or long-term career leverage.
excerpt: Introduction  AI changes software development most where work is repetitive, ambiguous, or documentationheavy. It does not remove the need for system thinking, review, debugging, or taste. The useful question for developers is not whether AI will replace the craft. It is how the craft changes when code generation becomes cheap.  This guide is a practical baseline for navigating that shift.   1. Keep the Core Job Description Clear

title: Photography for Technologists
description: A practical guide to bringing engineering discipline into photography through constraint framing, workflow design, metadata, and portfolio curation.
excerpt: Introduction  Photography gets stronger when it stops being treated as pure inspiration and starts being treated as a system of decisions. The goal is not to remove taste from the work. The goal is to create enough structure that your taste can show up consistently under real conditions.  This guide turns the article's ideas into an operating workflow you can actually use.   1. Define the Shot Before Touching the Camera

title: The Future of Payments & AI - Smarter, Faster, Safer for Everyone
description: A practical beginner’s guide to AI in payments, designed for SMBs and emerging merchants. Covers today’s AI use cases (fraud detection, routing, analytics), why they matter, and how businesses can prepare responsibly. Includes a checklist to help SMBs adopt AI-powered payment tools with confidence.
excerpt: Introduction  Artificial Intelligence (AI) is reshaping payments by making them smarter, faster, and safer. For small and midsized businesses (SMBs), AI lowers barriers to entry: powerful fraud prevention, automated invoicing, and realtime insights are now available through plugandplay payment solutions.    This guide explains what AI in payments means, where it shows up today, and how new businesses can adopt it responsibly.   1. What is AI in Payments?

title: Designing AI-Ready Interfaces
description: Practical guidance on prototyping, validating, and scaling AI-enhanced interfaces under VPDS.
excerpt: Purpose   This guide provides handson reference patterns, schemas, and workflows for designing and deploying AIready interfaces that meet Visa Product Design System (VPDS) standards.    By combining:    VPDS (design tokens, accessibility, compliance)    React + Tailwind + shadcn/ui (modern frontend scaffolding)    Hono APIs on Cloudflare Workers (edgenative backend)

title: AI-Powered Cloud Development
description: Hands-on patterns for building AI-augmented apps with React, Hono, and Cloudflare Workers.
excerpt: Overview This guide walks through creating a modern cloudnative app using:   React → UI layer    Hono → lightweight backend framework    Cloudflare Workers → deploy globally at the edge    pnpm → modern package manager

title: Vibe Coding for Game Dev
description: A practical guide to using AI-assisted prototyping in game development without losing system clarity, testability, or the path to production.
excerpt: Introduction  AI can make game prototyping dramatically faster, but speed is only useful if the prototype stays legible. This guide is about turning "vibe coding" from a loose intuition into a usable workflow for RTS, simulation, and systemsheavy concepts.   1. Decide What the Prototype Is Testing  Before generating assets, UI, or mission text, write down the actual question:

title: AI System Instructions For Transaction Analysis Guide
description: Step-by-step guide for implementing structured AI instructions in payments. Covers schemas, testing, monitoring, and compliance considerations without external references.
excerpt: Introduction  This guide outlines how to implement structured system instructions in payment environments. The goal is to ensure AI delivers predictable, explainable results in fraud detection and transaction analysis while remaining flexible for iteration.   1. Define the Role & Boundaries  ```json

title: Systems Mapping for Product Teams
description: A hands-on guide to using systems mapping to clarify product goals and reduce risk.
excerpt: Introduction  Systems mapping is a visual way to understand how different parts of a product or organization interact. This guide covers:   Why systems mapping matters  How to create a simple map  Common pitfalls

title: Edge-First Thinking
description: A practical guide to designing applications with edge computing in mind, from caching strategies to data locality.
excerpt: What is EdgeFirst Thinking?  Edgefirst thinking means designing your application architecture with edge computing as a primary consideration, not an afterthought. It's about bringing computation and data closer to users.   Why Edge Matters  Traditional architectures route all requests through centralized servers. This creates:

title: Prompt Design for AI Agents
description: A practical guide to designing effective prompts for AI agents, with real-world examples.
excerpt: Introduction  Prompt design is the art and science of crafting instructions that guide AI agents to produce useful, reliable, and safe outputs. This guide covers:   The anatomy of a good prompt  Common pitfalls  Examples and antipatterns

title: Continuous Delivery for Teams
description: A step-by-step guide to implementing continuous delivery in product teams.
excerpt: Introduction  Continuous delivery (CD) is the practice of keeping your product deployable at all times. This guide covers:   The principles of CD  How to set up a CD pipeline  Common challenges and solutions
